reviewNotes: Contract is thorough and well-structured with 30 criteria covering all Sprint 16 features.
All criteria have specific, measurable thresholds aligned with the difficulty table from the user prompt.
Per-world enemy density limits (C-01 to C-05) correctly match: W1=2, W2=3, W3=4, W4=5, Citadel=5.
Per-world hazard percentage limits (C-07 to C-10) correctly match: W1=5%, W2=10%, W3=15%, W4+Citadel=20%.
Per-world platform width minimums (C-12 to C-14) correctly match: W1=3, W2+W3=2, W4+Citadel=1.
C-23 arithmetic is correct: 13 stages × (1 path + 4 arena + 5 difficulty) = 130 individual checks.
Implementation integrity criteria (C-24 to C-26) correctly ensure level data is modified rather than validator thresholds being loosened — this is a critical anti-cheat measure.
Regression criteria (C-27, C-28) correctly cover Sprint 15 boss tuning deliverables (vulnerabilityWindow, bossHP).
regressionSprints correctly lists all 15 prior sprints.

concerns.issue: C-22 and C-23 are near-duplicates — both assert 0 failures across all 13 stages and all categories. C-22 checks 'top-level aggregate counts' while C-23 counts 130 individual checks. Functionally identical outcome, but acceptable redundancy for a final sprint.
concerns.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. Redundancy provides defense-in-depth for the final deliverable.

concerns.issue: Summary criteria (C-06, C-11, C-15) are strict supersets of their per-world counterparts (C-01-C-05, C-07-C-10, C-12-C-14). If all per-world criteria pass, the summary criteria pass automatically.
concerns.recommendation: Acceptable. Summary criteria serve as a failsafe and make the 13/13 requirement explicit.

concerns.issue: Mechanics gating criteria use 'code-review' testMethod. These are inherently harder to automate — the evaluator must inspect level data to verify e.g. no water tiles exist before stage 2-3. This is feasible but depends on the evaluator's ability to read level data files.
concerns.recommendation: Acceptable. The evaluator can grep level data for water/ice tile IDs in the relevant stage ranges.

concerns.issue: FPS regression test requires loading into a stage and checking window.__gameDebug.fps. Test steps don't specify which stage or how to enter one programmatically.
concerns.recommendation: Acceptable. The evaluator can use the console to load any stage or observe FPS after calling runPlayabilityCheck(). If the game boots at all, the FPS check is feasible.

concerns.issue: Debug overlay test uses 'manual' testMethod. For a web-frontend project, Playwright-based interaction (key press D, verify overlay elements) would be more reliable.
concerns.recommendation: Acceptable for a regression check. The debug overlay was validated in Sprint 13 and Sprint 14. A lightweight re-check is sufficient.

featuresCovered: F25: Difficulty Curve Rebalancing (enemy/hazard tuning) — covered by C-01 through C-15, C-24 through C-26
F24 + F25: Integration validation — covered by C-19 through C-23
coverageAnalysis.regressionCoverage: All 15 prior sprints listed. Explicit regression criteria C-27 (vulnerabilityWindow), C-28 (bossHP), C-29 (FPS), C-30 (debug overlay) cover Sprint 15, 14, 13, and 12 respectively. Full regression registry will be checked during evaluation.

summary: Strong contract for the final sprint. 30 criteria provide comprehensive coverage of enemy density rebalancing, hazard percentage reduction, platform width enforcement, mechanics gating, and the critical 130/130 full-validation-pass deliverable. The anti-cheat criteria (C-24-C-26) ensuring level data modification rather than validator threshold changes are a smart addition. Minor redundancy between summary and per-world criteria is acceptable defense-in-depth for the final sprint. All thresholds match the difficulty table from the user prompt. ACCEPTED.
